title: Linux Foundation Lab Submission
description: Evidence template for Linux command-line, service, package, permissions, networking, and troubleshooting labs in ARIA.

required_evidence.label: System identity
required_evidence.description: Show hostname, current user, working directory, and target container or VM.

required_evidence.label: Commands used
required_evidence.description: List the exact commands used during the lab. Do not include passwords, tokens, or private keys.

required_evidence.label: Observations
required_evidence.description: Explain what the command output showed and why it mattered.

required_evidence.label: Validation
required_evidence.description: Show how you proved the issue was fixed or the task was completed.

required_evidence.label: Professional summary
required_evidence.description: Write a short help desk or sysadmin-style summary of the work completed.

completion_signals: student identifies the target Linux system
student provides command output or clear observations
student explains what changed or what was validated
student provides a professional final summary
safety_rules: Do not submit passwords, API keys, SSH private keys, session cookies, or tokens.
Do not run destructive commands unless the lab explicitly instructs it.
Use read-only validation commands before making changes.
Explain the reasoning behind each command.
